reforms.description: Lithuania aims to enhance the effectiveness of R&D tax incentives by clarifying the definition of R&D and improving awareness of the tax scheme, while also providing R&D support through a balanced combination of tax incentives and direct support to smaller innovative firms. Additionally, the country is developing its capital markets to alleviate financial constraints faced by firms, which will support investments in research and development (R&D) and technological innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to stimulate R&D investment and support innovative firms, which are expected to enhance productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been officially announced but are not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant overhaul of the R&D support system, combining tax incentives with direct funding.
reforms.source_quote: Encouraging the development of non-bank capital markets would help to reduce financial constraints and support productivity growth by facilitating investment in intangibles, research and development (R&D), and technological innovation.

reforms.description: Lithuania has launched a national lifelong learning platform serving as a one-stop shop for adult education, with 50% of the funding allocated to the development of digital and technological skills.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to improve the skill set of the workforce, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The platform represents a significant investment in adult education and skill development.
reforms.source_quote: Lithuania has also launched a national lifelong learning platform serving as a one-stop shop for adult education in 2024, and 50% of the available funding is planned to be allocated to the development of digital and technological skills.

reforms.description: Lithuania has invested into two new venture capital funds that involve private capital and management, aimed at fostering the development of venture capital for innovative start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in venture capital is expected to enhance the funding landscape for innovative start-ups, promoting entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The investment has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of new venture capital funds represents a significant step in supporting the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Lithuania has recently invested into two new venture capital funds where private capital and/or private management are involved.

reforms.description: Lithuania has allocated EUR 45 million from the 'New Generation Lithuania' Plan to fund new private charging stations for electric vehicles, aiming to increase the number of charging stations significantly by March 2026.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in charging infrastructure is expected to facilitate the adoption of electric vehicles, contributing to environmental sustainability and innovation in transportation.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding represents a significant investment in infrastructure to support the transition to electric vehicles.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Energy has allocated €45 million of the 'New Generation Lithuania' Plan to contribute to the funding of new private charging stations.

reforms.description: Lithuania is providing EUR 5,000 for the purchase of new electric vehicles and EUR 2,500 for second-hand ones, alongside VAT deductions for EVs below EUR 50,000, to stimulate demand for electric vehicles.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The financial incentives are designed to boost the adoption of electric vehicles, which supports innovation in the automotive sector and environmental goals.
reforms.status_evidence: The incentives have been officially announced but are not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The provision of financial incentives represents a significant effort to stimulate the electric vehicle market.
reforms.source_quote: The government currently offers EUR 5,000 for the purchase of a new EV, and EUR 2,500 for a second-hand one.

reforms.description: Lithuania introduced an individual learning account system in 2023, providing EUR 500 for participants to access continuing education and career guidance, aimed at improving adult education and addressing skill mismatches in the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The system has been successfully implemented since 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the learning account system represents a significant step in supporting adult education.
reforms.source_quote: In 2023, an individual learning account system was introduced, offering continuing learning programmes and career guidance.

reforms.description: A new Employer Box programme is planned under the Ministry of Education to encourage employer-driven training of employees, combining state and company funding for training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme aims to enhance workforce skills through employer-driven training, which is essential for innovation and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant initiative to support training and skill development in the workforce.
reforms.source_quote: An Employer Box, under the Ministry of Education, is planned to encourage employer-driven training of company employees.
